claim: Context engineering matured dramatically in 2025—the field went from prototype to production in ~6 months starting mid-2024, though it's still in early prototyping stage at scale
actionable: Expect context engineering to reach maturity and standardization by end of 2026; current systems still have room for optimization and scaling
source_title: [State of Context Engineering] Agentic RAG, Context Rot, MCP, Subagents — Nina Lopatina, Contextual

claim: Agentic RAG (with query reformulation into subqueries) is now the baseline—query decomposition improved performance so dramatically it became the standard approach
actionable: Default to agentic RAG with query decomposition; breaking user queries into sub-queries before retrieval outperforms single-query RAG
source_title: [State of Context Engineering] Agentic RAG, Context Rot, MCP, Subagents — Nina Lopatina, Contextual

claim: Context rot (information degradation in large context windows) is cited everywhere but industry benchmarks at real scale (100k+ documents, billions of tokens) remain rare
actionable: Test context rot on your actual data size; published benchmarks may not reflect your scale—measure performance at 700k tokens in context to see real impact
source_title: [State of Context Engineering] Agentic RAG, Context Rot, MCP, Subagents — Nina Lopatina, Contextual

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is both a driver and a flaw: it enables rapid prototyping but stuffs context windows with giant JSON tool definitions, causing context rot
actionable: Use MCP for prototyping; once validated, migrate to direct API calls to reduce tool definition overhead in context
source_title: [State of Context Engineering] Agentic RAG, Context Rot, MCP, Subagents — Nina Lopatina, Contextual

claim: Sub-agents with explicit turn limits and constraints are critical for large-scale context engineering—unlimited agency causes agents to search exhaustively and repeatedly check work, wasting tokens
actionable: Cap sub-agent iterations: set max turns and max checks for validation; force agents to return early rather than explore every possibility
source_title: [State of Context Engineering] Agentic RAG, Context Rot, MCP, Subagents — Nina Lopatina, Contextual

claim: Instruction-following re-rankers are critical for scaling retrieval across massive databases—they enable higher recall in initial retrieval while ensuring precision in the final context window
actionable: Use instruction-following re-rankers in your retrieval pipeline; they reduce context rot by filtering high-recall initial results down to high-precision final context
source_title: [State of Context Engineering] Agentic RAG, Context Rot, MCP, Subagents — Nina Lopatina, Contextual

claim: Benchmarks for context engineering are saturating faster than in other ML domains—solutions that reach human-level performance on benchmarks need to have errors in the gold dataset checked
actionable: If your system hits 100% benchmark accuracy, investigate whether the gold standard has errors; use this as a signal to validate
source_title: [State of Context Engineering] Agentic RAG, Context Rot, MCP, Subagents — Nina Lopatina, Contextual

claim: For code-specific context engineering, multimodal ingestion and document hierarchy are critical—retrieval pipelines must include filters, re-rankers, and hybrid search for best results
actionable: When indexing code, preserve document structure (file hierarchy, class/function boundaries); use hybrid search + re-ranking in retrieval
source_title: [State of Context Engineering] Agentic RAG, Context Rot, MCP, Subagents — Nina Lopatina, Contextual

claim: KV (key-value) caching should be used in multi-turn agent conversations; it improves both efficiency and performance by keeping context stable across turns
actionable: Enable KV caching for agent loops and multi-turn conversations to reduce latency and improve consistency
source_title: [State of Context Engineering] Agentic RAG, Context Rot, MCP, Subagents — Nina Lopatina, Contextual

claim: JEPA (and similar genetic prompt evolution approaches like ACE) should make incremental tweaks to prompts rather than rewriting from scratch—incremental evolution preserves performance better than full rewrites
actionable: When auto-tuning prompts, apply small mutations and test; avoid wholesale prompt rewrites which lose critical nuance
source_title: [State of Context Engineering] Agentic RAG, Context Rot, MCP, Subagents — Nina Lopatina, Contextual

claim: Context compression (intentional truncation) is still necessary because multi-turn conversations bloat context—models are improving internal compression but humans still benefit from explicit refresh points
actionable: For long conversations, plan to open fresh windows or explicitly compress context after ~5+ turns to maintain performance
source_title: [State of Context Engineering] Agentic RAG, Context Rot, MCP, Subagents — Nina Lopatina, Contextual
